meaning: adj.鍙繊鍙楃殑,鑳藉繊鑰愮殑
examples: I found the woman scarcely endurable.
As our typewriters are made of light and hard alloy they are both portable and endurable.
"The May 2010 storm event should have been an endurable, natural event at or below the 100-year flood plain, " the lawsuit said.
Listening to music with a driving beat made the time endurable.
